item.title: Today’s Wordle #1722: Hints and Answer for March 7, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Today’s tips and strategies can help you solve it faster and maintain your streak—if you’re open to a little extra help. So, if you’ve found yourself stuck with today’s Wordle, don’t worry, below you’ll find some helpful hints and even the answer for Wordle Game #1722 for March 7, 2026

To play today’s Wordle, head to the game’s website. If you want to go right to today’s answer without any tips or tricks, you can click right here.

IMPORTANT: The following information includes spoilers on today’s and yesterday’s editions of Wordle. If you don’t want the answer given to you, stop scrolling.

How To Play Wordle

Wordle is a simple but addictive word puzzle. Each day, players have to guess a five-letter word answer in no more than six attempts. After each guess:

  • Green letters are correct and in the right position.
  • Yellow letters are correct, but in the wrong position.
  • Gray letters are not in the word at all.

The goal is to guess the word in as few tries as possible. Daily puzzles keep players coming back for more, making it a fun brain exercise and a popular online trend.

Wordle can only be played on the New York Times website. There is currently no app for you to download the official game.

Wordle Hints For Today, March 7, 2026

The Wordle answer for game #1722 is a hard puzzle; there’s no question about it. According to the NY Times Wordle review, today’s answer takes an average of 5.6 out of 6 guesses. That makes it a half-guess easier than the 4.5-guess average from March 6.

General Wordle Hints For March 7

Without giving today’s Wordle answer completely away, here are a few helpful hints for today to get you started:

  • Hint 1: This word is often used when talking about trends or popularity.
  • Hint 2: It usually describes something that becomes widely favored for a period of time.
  • Hint 3: The contexts where it appears most are fashion, culture, or media.
  • Hint 4: You’re more likely to see this word in magazines or commentary than in everyday conversation.

Use these clues to narrow down your guesses and increase your chances of solving Wordle #1722 for March 7, 2026.

Starter Words For March 7, 2026

If you want help getting started without having today’s Wordle answer given to you, there are a few starter words worth trying that will help you figure out some included letters. A few starter words that may help are:

  • VOTER
  • VIOLA
  • ROGUE

Today’s Wordle Answer For Game #1722 On March 7, 2026

If you’ve tried everything, including using the above hints, and are still stuck, you can find the answer to today’s Wordle below.

Answer: VOGUE

If you solved today’s Wordle without our answer, congratulations! If not, take note of the best tips above for tomorrow’s puzzle, and check back daily for more hints.

What Does Today’s Wordle Answer Mean?

VOGUE is a noun that means “something in fashion, as at a particular time.”

Yesterday’s Wordle Answer

If you missed the previous game, here’s yesterday’s Wordle answer for Game #1721 on March 6, 2026

Answer: GUNKY

While not always the case, looking at previous words can sometimes give you insight into common patterns or letter usage in Wordle puzzles.

When Is The Next Wordle Game After #1722?

Wordle games are updated daily at 12 am or Midnight in your respective time zone. Information about the next game is often available in the hours before to give players a heads up. The game on March 8 will be Wordle Game #1723.

What Is Wordle?

Wordle is a daily word puzzle created by Josh Wardle. Initially developed as a prototype in 2013, it wasn’t fully released until 2021. The game challenges players to guess a five-letter word in six tries or fewer, using hints from each attempt to narrow down the answer. Its straightforward concept, accessibility, and the thrill of solving a new puzzle each day helped it surge in popularity by late 2021. The success of Wordle inspired numerous spin-offs and social media challenges, leading to its acquisition by The New York Times in early 2022.

For even more Insider Gaming delivered straight to your inbox, sign up for our weekly newsletter

Mike has been covering the gaming industry since 2012, and has reported on some of the largest events in the industry while also working as an investigative reporter. Outside of…

More from Mike Straw

MOBILE APP

item.title: League of Legends LEC Spring Primer 2026 Details Revealed
item.content:encoded:
Now that LEC Versus has wrapped and First Stand sits just around the corner, the league is wasting no time retaining momentum. The 2026 LEC Spring Split surfaces on March 28, marking the bold return of a fan-favorite format that has been a constant request.

This year’s circuit will feature two international ‘roadtrips’ that elevate the competitive experience for players and fans alike more than ever.

LEC Spring Primer – All You Need to Know

Teams will be offered more depth in the LEC Spring Primer this year as a multi-game series opens up. The regular season unfolds over seven weeks of matchups, all best-of-three, and culminates in a six-team playoff bracket composed of a best-of-five rotation. There are two MSI qualification spots on the line, so the stakes are pretty high.

One of the key talking points of the LEC Spring Primer 2026 is the imminent arrival of Roadtrips. These will bring the LEC action closer to regional fanbases in both Spain and Paris. One Roadtrip will land in the Madrid Arena, while the other drops into Les Arenes de Grand Paris Sud.

Both of these LAN tour events boast three days of competitive action, and fans are welcome to attend the entire weekend spree.

Riot outlined the later schedule in a press release:

Playoffs will start on May 23 at 17:00 CEST with gamedays from Saturday to Monday each week with one Best-of-Five each day. The lower bracket finals and MSI qualification decider will be on June 6 and the Split Finals will be on June 7. 

In Berlin, and at the heart of the LEC Spring Primer in the Riot Games Arena, regular season matches will span Saturday to Monday, but Monday’s fixtures will be studio-only events. It might sound sad for some that the doors are closed for a portion of the event, but Rio has cited lower attendance and rising production costs as a factor behind the decision.

Tickets for the Spring Split are available now, and more Roadtrip information will be dropped very soon.

Are you looking forward to the League of Legends LEC Spring Primer 2026? Let us know on the Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more Insider Gaming coverage, check out the news that another Fortnite x Cyberpunk crossover is coming

Written by

Senior Editor and Esports Lead

Grant's adoration for gaming started on the Amstrad CPC in 1996. Now, he's a multi-platform expert gamer with a deep love for the industry, a passion for esports, and more…

More from Grant Taylor-Hill

item.title: Eglato Wave Link 3.0 Brings Major New Features To macOS
item.content:encoded:
For years, macOS users have often felt left out when new software gets released. Developing for Apple’s operating system comes with different challenges than Windows, and, for some, it’s viewed as not being worth the time. For Elgato, they know there’s a user base that would appreciate additional flexibility, especially when it comes to audio. That’s why Elgato is bringing a major new feature to macOS users with Wave Link 3.0.

Announced this week, Wave Link 3.0 is the next step for the company’s audio mixing software. A tool designed to bring “broadcast-level routing and effects without a steep learning curve or workarounds”, Wave Link lets you mix your audio at a level that you used to have to shell out money for.

The software lets you choose what you want to hear, what you want your audience to hear, and at what levels. Up until now, you had to have the software front and center to make those adjustments. In Wave Link 3.0 for macOS, you can now set your per-app and input volume control right within the macOS menu bar.

Before this feature, users needed to install open source options that could be daunting if you’ve never gone that route. With this, it’s all built into the software, meaning no extra add-ons or work is necessary.

This makes changing audio levels quick and easy. To some, it might not seem like that big of a deal. To others, it’s sure to be a game-changer in accessibility and ease of use.

Aside from the menu bar audio adjustments, Wave Link 3.0 brings with it Siri and Shortcuts integrations, a new Liquid Glass UI, and the brand new Wave Next that gives users control over their onboard DSP effects.

Wave Link 3.0 is now available for free on both macOS and Windows.

For more Insider Gaming delivered to your inbox, subscribe to the Insider Gaming Newsletter.

Mike has been covering the gaming industry since 2012, and has reported on some of the largest events in the industry while also working as an investigative reporter. Outside of…

item.title: Nintendo Suing United States Government Over Tariff
item.content:encoded:
As first reported by Nicole Carpenter of Aftermath, Nintendo of America is suing the United States government over the broad tariff policy enacted by President Donald Trump in 2025. The complaint, filed in the U.S. Court of International Trade, claims that since February 2025, the Trump Administration has imposed unlawful tariffs, leading to the collection of over $200 billion in tariffs on imports from nearly all countries.

In the complaint, Nintendo‘s legal counsel says the gaming company has “standing to sue” because it imports Goods that were subject to IEEPA Duties. Therefore, they’re seeking a refund with interest on the tariffs they paid. However, a specific amount was not stated. Alternatively, they would want to enter a money judgment for the amount. They’re also asking for reasonable attorney fees.

More on the Tariffs and Nintendo

On February 20, the US Supreme Court found the tariffs to be unconstitutional. Subsequently, New York Judge Richard Eaton of the U.S. Court of International Trade stated that all importers of record were entitled to benefit following the ruling. Eaton said he will be the one hearing cases involving refunds.

President Trump later said that he would impose new 15% tariffs, an increase from the previous tariffs.

Nintendo launched its Switch 2 console shortly after the tariffs were announced, and the tariffs actually delayed United States pre-orders for the hardware.

Those interested can read Nintendo’s full complaint at this link. Insider Gaming will continue to bring you the latest on this story as more develops.

What do you think about this move that sees Nintendo of America sue the United States government? Do you think the price of the Nintendo Switch 2 will rise in the near future due to tariff concerns and /or RAM issues? Sound off in our official Discord server. Also, don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.
